sourceFiles.name: editor (current)

results.name: Completeness
results.rawText:
Model Characterisation
- System purpose: A smart home monitoring/control system combining thermostat, lighting, and security camera elements for home occupants or operators.
- Operational domain: Consumer/home automation / residential IoT.
- Lifecycle phase: Early concept to preliminary design. The model contains high-level structure and one abstract behaviour, but lacks detailed interfaces, requirements, constraints, and verification content.
- Model scope: A partial system model fragment covering selected structure and one behaviour definition.
- Known scope limitations: No explicit requirements package, no context/boundary view, no interfaces/ports, no state machines, no allocations, no scenarios, and no verification artefacts are present in the provided model text. These are treated as missing where they would normally be expected for completeness unless the check depends on unavailable external artefacts.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-001]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          1. Requirements Coverage
Finding:        No stakeholder-level requirements are present in the model as `requirement` definitions.
Evidence:       The package contains only `attribute def`, `enum def`, `part def`, and `action def` elements; there are no `requirement` definitions anywhere in the provided model text.
Score:          0.90  -  Absence of requirements blocks traceability and prevents completeness and verification assessment of intended system behaviour.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Design decisions cannot be validated against stated needs, and downstream satisfaction/verification analysis is blocked.
Recommendation: Add stakeholder and system requirements as `requirement` definitions with text bodies, identified subjects, and trace links to design and verification elements.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-002]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          1. Requirements Coverage
Finding:        Because no requirements are defined, requirement descriptions, subjects, and `satisfy`/`verify` relationships are all missing.
Evidence:       No `requirement` elements, no `doc` text on requirements, and no `satisfy` or `verify` relationships appear in the model.
Score:          0.88  -  Missing requirement content and trace links makes it highly likely that necessary design obligations will be omitted or unverifiable.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Requirement allocation and verification coverage cannot be established.
Recommendation: For each requirement, add descriptive text, define the constrained subject, and link it to satisfying structural/behavioural elements and verification cases.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-003]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          1. Requirements Coverage
Finding:        No derived requirements or parent-child requirement relationships are present.
Evidence:       The model contains no `derivedReq`, refinement, or any requirement hierarchy constructs.
Score:          0.55  -  Derived design obligations are likely to be missed once the system is elaborated.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  This assumes the model is intended to support more than a minimal concept sketch; a very early fragment may intentionally omit derivation.
Impact:         Lower-level subsystem requirements for thermostat, lighting, and camera functions cannot be traced back to system intent.
Recommendation: Add derived requirements for subsystem-specific behaviour and relate them to parent requirements using `derivedReq` or refinement.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-004]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          1. Requirements Coverage
Finding:        Non-functional requirements such as performance, safety, reliability, maintainability, and interoperability are absent.
Evidence:       No requirements of any kind are present, and no elements express quantitative or quality constraints for the system.
Score:          0.72  -  Smart-home systems depend on non-functional properties for safe and usable operation; omission is likely to lead to incomplete design and verification.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  Severity depends on intended project maturity and whether such requirements are maintained externally.
Impact:         Critical quality attributes cannot be analysed or verified, including sensing accuracy, response time, availability, and security-related expectations.
Recommendation: Add explicit non-functional requirements and trace them to attributes, constraints, and verification methods.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-005]
Element:        SmartHome::SmartHomeSystem
Check:          2. System Boundary
Finding:        The model has a top-level system definition, but external actors, external systems, and environment elements are not identified or modelled.
Evidence:       `part def SmartHomeSystem { part thermostat : Thermostat; part lights : LightController[1..*]; part cameras : SecurityCamera[2..4]; }` contains only internal parts; no user, homeowner, mobile app, power supply, network, or environment context elements are shown.
Score:          0.78  -  Missing external context is likely to cause omission of boundary interactions and environmental assumptions.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Context analysis, interface identification, and use-case completeness are undermined.
Recommendation: Add context-level parts/actors representing users, external services, utilities, networks, and relevant environment elements outside the system boundary.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-006]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          2. System Boundary
Finding:        No context diagram or equivalent construct makes the system boundary explicit.
Evidence:       The model includes only internal type definitions and one action; there is no context assembly or boundary-specific representation.
Score:          0.68  -  Boundary ambiguity can lead to wrong assumptions about responsibilities and missing interfaces.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         System-of-interest scope and external interaction analysis cannot be performed reliably.
Recommendation: Add a context view showing the SmartHomeSystem and all external actors/systems with explicit boundary crossings.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-007]
Element:        SmartHome::SmartHomeSystem
Check:          2. System Boundary
Finding:        External interfaces crossing the system boundary are not identified as ports or connections on the boundary element.
Evidence:       `part def SmartHomeSystem` declares only internal parts and no `port` or `connect` elements.
Score:          0.84  -  Without boundary interfaces, integration and interface analysis are likely to fail.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         It is impossible to determine how the system communicates with users, sensors, networks, power, or external services.
Recommendation: Define ports on `SmartHomeSystem` for all external interactions and connect them to internal parts or interface definitions.

CHECK PASSED: 3. Structural Decomposition  -  The model defines a top-level `SmartHomeSystem` with typed internal parts (`thermostat`, `lights`, `cameras`), and all instantiated parts are typed by `part def` elements appropriate for a high-level preliminary structural view.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-008]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          4. Interface & Connection Completeness
Finding:        No ports are defined on any part definition, so direction and flow typing for interfaces are entirely missing.
Evidence:       `Sensor`, `Thermostat`, `LightController`, `SecurityCamera`, and `SmartHomeSystem` contain attributes and parts only; no `port` declarations appear anywhere.
Score:          0.89  -  Missing ports prevents interface definition, flow analysis, and integration modelling.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Internal and external communication pathways cannot be analysed or validated.
Recommendation: Add ports to each interacting part with explicit direction and typed flows/items.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-009]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          4. Interface & Connection Completeness
Finding:        No connections are defined between internal parts, leaving all potential interactions unconnected in the system assembly.
Evidence:       `part def SmartHomeSystem` contains part usages only and no `connect` statements between thermostat, lights, cameras, or any external interfaces.
Score:          0.86  -  Unmodelled connectivity is highly likely to produce incomplete or incorrect integration assumptions.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Signal/energy/data paths between subsystems cannot be traced, analysed, or verified.
Recommendation: Add explicit `connect` relationships between communicating parts and between parts and system boundary ports.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-010]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          4. Interface & Connection Completeness
Finding:        No flow definitions or interface definitions are present for significant subsystem interactions.
Evidence:       The model contains no `flow`, `item def`, interface constructs, or typed interaction specifications for temperature data, lighting commands, or video data.
Score:          0.80  -  Without typed flows, interaction semantics are too incomplete for design or analysis.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Interface compatibility, bandwidth, command/data semantics, and integration contracts remain undefined.
Recommendation: Define item/interface types for sensed temperature, brightness commands, video streams, device status, and other significant exchanges.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-011]
Element:        SmartHome::MonitorHome
Check:          5. Behaviour Completeness
Finding:        Only one behavioural model is present, and there is no evidence that every operationally significant function has its own behaviour model.
Evidence:       The sole behaviour is `action def MonitorHome`; no separate actions, state machines, or sequences are defined for thermostat control, lighting control, security monitoring, startup, shutdown, or fault handling.
Score:          0.74  -  Important system functions are likely underspecified, leading to incomplete behavioural understanding.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  What counts as "operationally significant" depends partly on project intent, though the model text suggests multiple functions beyond the single action.
Impact:         Functional analysis and operational validation are incomplete.
Recommendation: Add behaviour models for each major function and mode, such as temperature regulation, lighting adjustment, video monitoring, alarm handling, and system lifecycle operations.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-012]
Element:        SmartHome::MonitorHome::checkTemperature; SmartHome::MonitorHome::adjustLights; SmartHome::MonitorHome::recordVideo
Check:          5. Behaviour Completeness
Finding:        Referenced sub-actions are declared without typed inputs/outputs or bodies, making them behavioural stubs.
Evidence:       Inside `action def MonitorHome`: `action checkTemperature; action adjustLights; action recordVideo;` with no parameter typing or internal behaviour shown.
Score:          0.77  -  Stub actions are likely to conceal missing functional detail and block behavioural analysis.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Data dependencies, control logic, and responsibility assignment for the core functions cannot be assessed.
Recommendation: Define each action explicitly with typed inputs/outputs and either a body or delegation to more detailed sub-actions.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-013]
Element:        SmartHome::MonitorHome
Check:          5. Behaviour Completeness
Finding:        The behaviour covers only a nominal sequential monitoring flow; operational scenarios and modes are not represented.
Evidence:       `first checkTemperature; then adjustLights; then recordVideo;` is the only behavioural sequencing present; there are no alternate paths, modes, or event-driven behaviours.
Score:          0.69  -  Missing operational modes can lead to decisions based on incomplete behavioural assumptions.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Mode logic such as idle, armed, standby, manual override, and degraded operation cannot be analysed.
Recommendation: Add state machines or additional actions/scenarios to represent operational modes, branching conditions, and event responses.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-014]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          6. Value & Constraint Completeness
Finding:        Attribute definitions are untyped with no value types or units, so quantitative semantics are missing.
Evidence:       `attribute def Temperature; attribute def Percentage; attribute def DeviceName;` provide names only and do not specify value type, dimension, or units.
Score:          0.83  -  Missing value typing and units is likely to cause analytical ambiguity and incorrect interpretation of data.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Quantitative analysis of temperature and brightness values cannot be performed consistently.
Recommendation: Type each attribute definition with appropriate scalar or custom value types and attach units where applicable, e.g. degrees Celsius and percent.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-015]
Element:        SmartHome::Thermostat::targetTemp; SmartHome::Thermostat::currentTemp; SmartHome::LightController::brightness
Check:          6. Value & Constraint Completeness
Finding:        Critical numerical attributes lack bounds such as minimum, maximum, or nominal values.
Evidence:       `attribute targetTemp : Temperature; attribute currentTemp : Temperature; attribute brightness : Percentage;` are present without any range or limit information.
Score:          0.63  -  Missing bounds weakens design and safety analysis but may be tolerable in a very early concept model.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  Expected level of numeric detail depends on lifecycle intent, though these are clearly important controllable/measured values.
Impact:         Valid operating envelopes and requirement conformance cannot be checked.
Recommendation: Add range constraints or attribute metadata for allowable and nominal temperature and brightness values.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-016]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          6. Value & Constraint Completeness
Finding:        No constraint definitions are present for quantitative behaviour or performance.
Evidence:       The model contains no `constraint` or `constraint def` elements for control limits, timing, energy, storage, or other measurable properties.
Score:          0.71  -  Quantitative requirements cannot be formalised or analysed without constraints.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Performance budgeting and parametric evaluation are blocked.
Recommendation: Add constraint definitions for key quantitative properties such as thermostat control tolerance, lighting range, camera retention/storage, and response times.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-017]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          6. Value & Constraint Completeness
Finding:        No parametric bindings connect constraints to actual attribute values in a usage context.
Evidence:       No constraint usages, binding connectors, or parametric contexts appear in the model.
Score:          0.58  -  Lack of bindings prevents quantitative execution of any future constraints.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Even if requirements are later added, there is currently no path to model-based quantitative analysis.
Recommendation: Introduce parametric contexts binding system and subsystem attributes to constraint parameters.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-018]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          7. Allocation Completeness
Finding:        No allocation relationships are present between functional and physical elements.
Evidence:       The model contains one action definition and several part definitions but no `allocate` relationships.
Score:          0.67  -  Missing allocation creates uncertainty about which components realize which functions.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Traceability from functions to implementing components is absent, hindering design responsibility and impact analysis.
Recommendation: Allocate behavioural functions such as temperature checking, light adjustment, and video recording to physical parts or logical hosts.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-019]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          7. Allocation Completeness
Finding:        If software functions are intended, they are not allocated to any execution platform or hosting element.
Evidence:       `MonitorHome` and its sub-actions exist as functions, but there is no controller, processor, hub, or platform element and no allocations to such an element.
Score:          0.60  -  Platform ambiguity can lead to incomplete implementation planning.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  The model does not explicitly distinguish software from abstract functional behaviour, so this depends on intended architecture.
Impact:         Feasibility, deployment, and resource ownership for control logic cannot be assessed.
Recommendation: Add execution-platform elements where relevant and allocate control/monitoring actions to them.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-020]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          7. Allocation Completeness
Finding:        No interface allocations to communication media or logical zones are modelled.
Evidence:       There are no interfaces, communication media, zones, or associated allocation relationships in the package.
Score:          0.42  -  This reduces architectural traceability but is secondary to the more fundamental missing interface model.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  Relevance depends on whether communication architecture and zoning are in scope at this stage.
Impact:         Communication-path and installation-location reasoning cannot be traced in the model.
Recommendation: Once interfaces are defined, allocate them to Wi‑Fi, wired links, or other media and allocate components to rooms/zones if relevant.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-021]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          8. Scenario & Use Case Coverage
Finding:        No mission or operational scenario is defined that exercises the full system in context.
Evidence:       The model contains one abstract action definition but no use cases, scenarios, interaction sequences, or context-driven operational narratives.
Score:          0.73  -  Missing end-to-end scenarios is likely to leave interactions and mode coverage incomplete.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Stakeholder validation and operational thread analysis cannot be performed.
Recommendation: Add at least one end-to-end operational scenario covering sensing, decision logic, actuation, and surveillance in context.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-022]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          8. Scenario & Use Case Coverage
Finding:        Startup, shutdown, and transition sequences are not explicitly modelled.
Evidence:       No behaviours, states, or scenarios reference initialization, power-on, shutdown, or transitions between modes.
Score:          0.61  -  Lifecycle transitions are common failure points and their omission leaves important behaviour unspecified.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Operational readiness and safe transitions cannot be assessed.
Recommendation: Add behaviours or state transitions for startup, shutdown, and changes between operational modes.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-023]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          8. Scenario & Use Case Coverage
Finding:        Off-nominal or degraded scenarios are absent.
Evidence:       The only behaviour is a nominal sequence in `MonitorHome`; there are no fault, safe-state, communication-loss, sensor-failure, or manual-override scenarios.
Score:          0.76  -  Excluding off-nominal behaviour is likely to hide safety and resilience gaps.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Fault handling, recovery behaviour, and degraded operation cannot be validated.
Recommendation: Add off-nominal scenarios and state-based responses for device failure, invalid readings, network loss, and safe fallback behaviour.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-024]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          9. Verification Methods
Finding:        No requirements have defined verification methods because no requirements or verification annotations are present.
Evidence:       The model contains no `requirement` elements and no verification method statements such as test, analysis, demonstration, or inspection.
Score:          0.91  -  Verification planning is effectively impossible without requirement-method assignments.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         The model cannot support verification strategy definition or audit readiness.
Recommendation: For each requirement, define a verification method and capture it in the model or through linked verification artefacts.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-025]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          9. Verification Methods
Finding:        No verification cases or test scenarios are present or linked to model elements.
Evidence:       No test cases, verification cases, scenarios, or `verify` relationships appear anywhere in the model text.
Score:          0.87  -  Lack of verification cases makes it highly likely that coverage gaps will persist into integration and acceptance.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Requirement verification coverage and evidence planning cannot be assessed.
Recommendation: Add verification/test case definitions and link them to requirements and relevant structural/behavioural elements.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-026]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          9. Verification Methods
Finding:        It is unclear which model elements would serve as evidence for any verification activity.
Evidence:       There are no explicit verification relationships, evidence links, or traceability paths from requirements to behaviour, structure, and tests.
Score:          0.79  -  Missing evidence traceability is likely to cause verification incompleteness and review failure.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Verification closure and certification/audit-style traceability cannot be demonstrated.
Recommendation: Establish end-to-end traceability from requirements to satisfying design elements, verification cases, and evidence-producing model artefacts.

Summary Table

| ID | Score | Confidence | One-line description |
|---|---:|---|---|
| COMPLETENESS-001 | 0.90 | HIGH | No stakeholder-level requirements are present in the model. |
| COMPLETENESS-002 | 0.88 | HIGH | Requirement text, subjects, and satisfy/verify links are all missing. |
| COMPLETENESS-003 | 0.55 | MEDIUM | No derived requirements or requirement hierarchy is defined. |
| COMPLETENESS-004 | 0.72 | MEDIUM | Non-functional requirements are absent. |
| COMPLETENESS-005 | 0.78 | HIGH | External actors/systems/environment are not modelled. |
| COMPLETENESS-006 | 0.68 | HIGH | No explicit context diagram or equivalent system boundary view is present. |
| COMPLETENESS-007 | 0.84 | HIGH | External boundary interfaces are not identified as ports or connections. |
| COMPLETENESS-008 | 0.89 | HIGH | No ports are defined on any part, so interface directions and flow types are missing. |
| COMPLETENESS-009 | 0.86 | HIGH | No connections are defined between internal parts. |
| COMPLETENESS-010 | 0.80 | HIGH | No flow or interface definitions are present for subsystem interactions. |
| COMPLETENESS-011 | 0.74 | MEDIUM | Behaviour is not provided for all operationally significant functions. |
| COMPLETENESS-012 | 0.77 | HIGH | Referenced sub-actions are behavioural stubs without typed parameters or bodies. |
| COMPLETENESS-013 | 0.69 | HIGH | Only a nominal sequential behaviour is modelled; modes/scenarios are missing. |
| COMPLETENESS-014 | 0.83 | HIGH | Attribute definitions lack value typing and units. |
| COMPLETENESS-015 | 0.63 | MEDIUM | Critical numerical attributes lack bounds. |
| COMPLETENESS-016 | 0.71 | HIGH | No quantitative constraint definitions are present. |
| COMPLETENESS-017 | 0.58 | HIGH | No parametric bindings connect constraints to attribute values. |
| COMPLETENESS-018 | 0.67 | HIGH | No allocations connect functional elements to physical elements. |
| COMPLETENESS-019 | 0.60 | MEDIUM | Functional behaviour is not allocated to any execution platform. |
| COMPLETENESS-020 | 0.42 | MEDIUM | No interface/media or zone allocations are modelled. |
| COMPLETENESS-021 | 0.73 | HIGH | No end-to-end operational scenario is defined. |
| COMPLETENESS-022 | 0.61 | HIGH | Startup, shutdown, and transition sequences are not modelled. |
| COMPLETENESS-023 | 0.76 | HIGH | Off-nominal/degraded scenarios are absent. |
| COMPLETENESS-024 | 0.91 | HIGH | No verification methods are defined for requirements. |
| COMPLETENESS-025 | 0.87 | HIGH | No verification cases or test scenarios are present. |
| COMPLETENESS-026 | 0.79 | HIGH | No traceability identifies model elements as verification evidence. |

Overall Assessment Score: 0.26

Model Status for this Dimension: Critical deficiencies  -  major rework required before this model can be used.
